blocks.content: Sophie was in the doorway. She had seen enough. Wren stood just behind her with one hand pressed flat to the wall like she needed something solid.
Nadia came last. She stepped into the room, looked at Marcus in the bed, and stood very still.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I mean it. Don't touch anything. We need to call somebody.
His voice was steady. The kind of steady that costs something.
He said it once. That was enough.
Cade straightened up from the bed. His hands were shaking. He pressed them together in front of him like he was trying to hold himself still.
Sophie made a sound in the doorway — not words, just sound, the kind that comes before words when words haven't caught up yet.
The window was open all the way. The curtain moved. Outside, the ocean was doing what the ocean always does.
Nobody answered. It wasn't a question anyone could answer.
On the nightstand: a glass of water, half empty. His phone, face-down. His watch. Nothing else.
